title: It's Election Day in Indiana. These key races are the ones to watch.
shortDescription: From a six-way competition for governor to open seats in Congress, here are the races to watch in Tuesday's primary.

title: Trump trial, Day 13: Stormy Daniels could reportedly testify Tuesday
shortDescription: Follow live updates as Donald Trump faces new testimony on his alleged cover-up of a hush money scheme to unlawfully interfere with an election.

title: Biden's Holocaust memorial speech holds key to presidential future
shortDescription: Biden faces commemorating the biggest massacre of Jewish people at a time when Jewish students on college campuses are feeling unsafe.

title: Guilty? By nearly 3-1, Americans predict Trump conviction in NY trial
shortDescription: Public opinion won't determine the trial's outcome, but it will affect the political repercussions once it's over.

title: How Republicans have latched onto the college protests to upend 2024
shortDescription: A timeline showcasing how presidential politics has intersected with the Middle East, from Oct. 7 through the latest bid for a ceasefire in Gaza.

title: A look at how Biden and Trump are polling in swing states
shortDescription: With just 26 weeks until Election Day on Nov. 5, we look at polling numbers for Biden and Trump in key battle states.

title: In Detroit visit, VP Harris touts Biden admin's economic policies
shortDescription: The stop, part of the vice president economic opportunity tour, drew pro-Palestinian protesters who decried the Biden administration's Gaza policies.

title: NH bill to raise marriage age to 18 passes, sent to Gov. Sununu
shortDescription: “Child marriage under any circumstance is not in the best interest of the child,” said NH Rep. Cassandra Levesque, D-Barrington.

title: White House knocks Kristi Noem for suggesting Joe Biden's dog should be killed
shortDescription: The White House knocked Kristi Noem after she took aim at President Joe Biden’s dog, Commander, suggesting that he should have been killed.

title: Trump trial witness list: Who has testified and who is still expected?
shortDescription: Hope Hicks and David Pecker have testified in Donald Trump's hush money trial. Stormy Daniels hasn't. Here's who else is left.

title: No, Congress isn't voting on a bill to ban 9/11 questions | Fact check
shortDescription: No such bills are listed on legislation tracking websites, nor are there credible reports of such legislation from legitimate news outlets.

title: What is a swing state? These purple places can help swing an election.
shortDescription: States that have a history of flipping red or blue depending on an election are considered battleground or swing states.

title: Who will Donald Trump pick as running mate? | The Excerpt
shortDescription: Plus: Jobs news, protesters pushing back against book bans, Presidential Medal of Freedom awards. It's all on The Excerpt.

title: Is Virginia flippable? Trump seems to think so
shortDescription: The former president still hopes to win Virginia in November, despite losing the commonwealth in 2016 and 2020.

title: Fewer Black Americans say they will vote in 2024, new poll finds
shortDescription: Fewer Black Americans say they will show up to the polls this year when compared to 2020, a trend that could spell bad news for President Joe Biden.

title: OnPolitics: Biden and Trump tied in exclusive poll
shortDescription: Six months before Election Day, President Joe Biden and former President Donald Trump are tied 37%-37% in an exclusive USA TODAY/Suffolk University Poll.

title: Here's what we know now about election polling
shortDescription: In the 2016 presidential election, pollsters infamously predicted that Hillary Clinton would win the White House. Here's what we know now about election polling in 2024.

title: Did Sen. Tim Scott, a Trump ally, certify the 2020 election?
shortDescription: The U.S. Senator from the Palmetto State won't commit to accepting the results of the 2024 presidential election, but had no problem in 2020.

title: These six states might decide the 2024 presidential election
shortDescription: Roughly 6% of voters in those states will likely be deciders.

title: Trump threatened with jail for gag order violations after 10th fine
shortDescription: Former president Donald Trump was held in criminal contempt for violating a gag order when he spoke to the press about the jury. He must pay $1,000.

title: Bernie Sanders, 82, announces he will run again for Senate
shortDescription: The self-described democratic socialist would be 89 years old when his next Senate term ends.
